I focus on building systems with a cloud-native approach, using Kubernetes as a core component. I’m passionate about continuous learning and developing effective strategies for personal and professional growth.

person.name: Louis Gerhardus Ferreira
person.bio: I am a Kubernetes professional who has earned all five CNCF Kubernetes certifications, demonstrating comprehensive expertise across administration, application development, security, and cloud-native fundamentals. My experience focuses on designing, securing, and operating scalable Kubernetes platforms in real-world environments. I am passionate about cloud-native technologies, continuous learning, and applying best practices to build reliable, secure systems. I enjoy contributing to the broader Kubernetes and CNCF community through knowledge sharing and hands-on problem solving.

person.name: Ties Brobbel
person.bio: I love exploring new technologies, and you also see that reflected in my professional career. I started out in the GIS technologies, moved to .NET space as a C# developer, and recently switched to Kubernetes SRE, and absolutely love the space.  When WSL was launched, I loved having a linux prompt to my disposal again, as I used ilnux for ages, even running Gentoo for a while. When I learned about kubernetes I was facinated with all the intracacies of all the cloud native concepts part of that tech, which really highlighted how to run reliable business applications properly on a platform, as we tried to do in the .NET space, but wasn't always easy to apply resiliency, reliability concepts.  Personally, I enjoy hacking again on my dotfiles, learning about new CLIs and TUIs, and automating my house using Home Assistent. But that's only when I have some downtime from my family of 2 kids, and a dog ;)

person.name: Kuldeep Sharma
person.bio: I love technology in every aspect. It empowers us to do more, to do better. I strongly believe in Kubernetes and its potential to improve lifecycle management of applications as well as any custom resources in cloud.

person.name: Mustapha Ghazali
person.bio: As a solution architect , I help businesses improve and build the right product for the right user. With over 14 years of experience in web development, I have a deep understanding of the technical and business aspects of digital projects and products.My core competencies include cloud-native applications, Kubernetes, Docker, SRE, Drupal, and TPM.  I enjoy working with cross-functional teams to deliver holistic solutions that meet the needs and expectations of the customers and the stakeholders.  My goal is to enable digital transformation and innovation for your organization

person.name: Suresh Kaviraj
person.bio: DevOps engineer specializing in Kubernetes, configuration-as-code, containerized workloads, and automation. I focus on building reliable, scalable systems through metrics-driven monitoring and proactive alerting. I’m passionate about collaborating with product developers, SREs, security teams, and architects to define engineering standards, enforce best practices, and deliver platforms that are secure, resilient, and cost-efficient.

person.name: Anirudh Pasalapudi
person.bio: I am a cloud engineer with experience of 12 years. I moved to India from US to stay with my parents and explore opportunities here. I am expert in management and technology at the same time. I have two kids son and a daughter. Outside of work I love to read books.

person.name: Emmanuel Uribe
person.bio: I'm an SRE with experience managing Kubernetes clusters across multiple clouds and on-prem environments. I focus on building resilient, scalable infrastructure following DevOps best practices. Passionate about CNCF technologies, I enjoy improving platform reliability through automation and observability. I regularly work with tools like Helm, Argo CD, KEDA, and Prometheus to enhance deployment automation, scalability, and monitoring.

person.name: Rakesh Bommisetty
person.bio: Rakesh is a Fellow Cloud Architect at Pegasystems with over 17 years of experience designing and evolving large‑scale cloud platforms. He leads innovation in cloud services, including building AI‑powered tools that enhance client autonomy and operational efficiency.  As a member of the Architecture Guild, Rakesh is responsible for solution design, cloud architecture, and enterprise‑scale transformation initiatives. He ensures architectural compliance and guides design standards across major cloud programs, with a strong focus on scalable, resilient, and cloud‑native systems.

person.name: Tibor Petróczy
person.bio: I’m a platform engineer with a strong background in system and database administration, currently focusing on cloud-native technologies and infrastructure automation. My specialties include Kubernetes, Azure, GitHub Actions, Terraform, and CI/CD, with a deep interest in CNCF projects and GitOps methodologies. I actively work with IaC tools like Terraform and Ansible and develop in Bash, PowerShell, Python, and Go to streamline platform operations.

person.name: Jean Pimmel
person.bio: Jean Pimmel is a Computer Engineering graduate and Data Platform Engineer at BSC Analytics. Specialized in Kubernetes, Terraform, and observability tools, Jean helps customers by managing and optimizing enterprise-level cloud and Kubernetes environments, improving resilience and efficiency through cloud-native tools and best practices. Passionate about automation, Jean focuses on streamlining workflows, optimizing operations, and helping teams deploy applications reliably and at scale.

person.name: Wan, Jiun-Wei
person.bio: I am a seasoned SRE/DevOps expert with deep experience in building, optimizing, and scaling cloud-native infrastructures, especially on Google Cloud and Kubernetes. I specialize in managing high-traffic services, developing automation platforms with tools like ArgoCD and Kubernetes Operators, and have a proven track record in architecture optimization.My passion for technology drives me to actively contribute to open-source projects such as Knative Serving and OpenTelemetry Operator, complemented by certifications like CKA, CKAD, and CKS. Furthermore, I frequently share my expertise on observability, automation, and Kubernetes best practices as a speaker at various tech conferences, including LINE Dev Day, COSCUP, ithome SRE CONFERENCE and KCD.

person.name: Andini Anissa
person.bio: I'm Andini Anissa, a software engineer living in Jakarta, Indonesia. Currently, I work at Sivali Cloud Technology, where I am responsible for managing their internal web applications from development to production. On a daily basis, I use Golang, React, Kubernetes, Cilium, Trivy, Falco, and Istio.
